title: Interpret Tote Presence And Tote Mismatch From Robot Selection Details
summary: Use the OptiSweep Robot selection detail panel to interpret whether a selected robot is carrying a tote, shows a tote mismatch indicated by a prefixed question mark, or has no tote indicated by the absence of the brown box and the text "layer_1".
when_to_use: Use this reference when viewing a robot in the OptiSweep Robot view and you need to determine the tote state from the robot selection details.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as a corrective or recovery procedure for removing or re-adding robots or totes.
Do not invent corrective actions beyond the documented meaning that a '?' indicates the robot was removed but the tote was not.
Do not use this runbook if the tote display does not match any of the documented patterns.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Select the robot, inspect the robot detail panel, and interpret the tote state using only the documented indicators.

safety_notes: Use only the documented display meanings from the source.
Do not infer undocumented corrective actions from the tote indicators.
Escalate if the display does not match the documented patterns.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the OptiSweep Robot view
Visual access to the robot selection display

access_required: Access to the OptiSweep Robot view
system_preconditions: A robot can be selected in the Robot view so its details are shown.
The robot detail panel is visible or expanded.

visual_references.description: Robot selection overview frame showing the robot detail panel and tote indicator meanings, including tote text under robot ID, '?' prefix meaning, and no-tote indication.

steps.title: Select the robot in Robot view
steps.purpose: Display the selected robot's details so the tote indicators can be interpreted.
steps.instruction: In the Robot view, select the robot you want to inspect so its details are shown. If the detail section is hidden, expand it using the small arrow.
steps.expected_result: The selected robot is highlighted and its detail panel is visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected robot has a box around it and the robot detail section is visible.
steps.failure_condition: The robot cannot be selected or the detail section cannot be viewed.

actions.label: Select robot
actions.exact_action: Click on a robot to view robot details.

actions.label: Expand details if hidden
actions.exact_action: If the detail section is hidden, use the small arrow to expand it.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Selected robot outline/box and the expandable robot detail section.

stop_or_escalate_if: The robot detail panel is not available after selection.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a robot is selected, you can click on a robot and see details. If the detail section is hidden, a small arrow expands it. The selected robot has a box around it.

steps.title: Check for tote text under the robot ID
steps.purpose: Use the text field under the robot ID as the primary tote-state indicator.
steps.instruction: Look under the robot ID in the detail panel for a tote identifier.
steps.expected_result: You can see whether tote text is present under the robot ID.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote field is visible and readable under the robot ID.
steps.failure_condition: The tote field is missing, unreadable, or does not match documented patterns.

actions.label: Inspect tote field
actions.exact_action: Look under the robot ID for a tote identifier.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The text area under the robot ID where the tote identifier appears.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote display does not match any documented pattern.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the robot has a tote it will show under the robot ID.

steps.title: Interpret a displayed tote ID as tote present
steps.purpose: Determine when the selected robot is carrying a tote.
steps.instruction: If a tote ID is shown under the robot ID, interpret that as the robot having a tote on it.
steps.expected_result: The robot is classified as carrying a tote when a tote ID is displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: A tote ID is shown under the robot ID and is interpreted as tote present.
steps.failure_condition: A tote ID is shown but the display also appears inconsistent with the documented indicators.

actions.label: Interpret tote ID
actions.exact_action: If a tote ID is shown, interpret that as the robot having a tote on it.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: A tote identifier displayed under the robot ID.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed tote state conflicts with the documented visual indicators.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the robot has a tote it will show under the robot ID.

steps.title: Interpret a prefixed question mark as tote mismatch after robot removal
steps.purpose: Identify the documented mismatch condition where the robot was removed but the tote was not.
steps.instruction: Check whether the tote text has a '?' before it. If present, interpret this as the robot having been removed while the tote has not been removed.
steps.expected_result: A tote ID prefixed with '?' is recognized as the documented removed-robot/tote-not-removed condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The '?' prefix is present and interpreted only as the documented mismatch meaning.
steps.failure_condition: The display suggests a mismatch state that is not covered by the documented '?' meaning.

actions.label: Inspect question mark prefix
actions.exact_action: Check whether the tote text has a '?' before it.

actions.label: Interpret mismatch meaning
actions.exact_action: If '?' is present, interpret this as the robot having been removed but the tote has not been removed.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote text under the robot ID and whether a '?' appears before the tote identifier.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote display does not match any documented pattern.
A corrective action is needed beyond the documented interpretation.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the tote has a '?' before it, the robot has been removed but the tote has not.

steps.title: Use the brown box on the robot image as the tote-present visual cue
steps.purpose: Confirm tote presence using the documented visual indicator on the robot image.
steps.instruction: Observe whether the robot image shows the small brown box on top. Use that as the visual cue that the AGV has a tote.
steps.expected_result: The brown box visual is used as a tote-present indicator.
steps.healthy_condition: The small brown box is visible on top of the AGV when a tote is present.
steps.failure_condition: The visual state does not align with the documented tote text indicators.

actions.label: Inspect brown box indicator
actions.exact_action: Observe whether the robot image shows the small brown box on top; use that as the visual cue that the AGV has a tote.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The robot image and the small brown box on top of the AGV indicating tote presence.

stop_or_escalate_if: The brown box visual does not match the documented tote text state.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If a robot does not have a tote it will say layer_1, and the brown box is not shown.
Artifact explains tote handling and robot selection overview, including tote indicators in the robot detail panel.

steps.title: Interpret no tote from layer_1 and no brown box
steps.purpose: Determine when the selected robot is not carrying a tote.
steps.instruction: If no tote is being carried, verify that the brown box is not shown and the tote section says 'layer_1'.
steps.expected_result: The robot is classified as having no tote when 'layer_1' is shown and the brown box is absent.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote section says 'layer_1' and the brown box is not shown.
steps.failure_condition: The display shows neither a tote ID nor the documented no-tote pattern of 'layer_1' with no brown box.

actions.label: Verify no-tote pattern
actions.exact_action: Verify that the brown box is not shown and the tote section says 'layer_1'.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote section showing 'layer_1' and the robot image without the brown box.

stop_or_escalate_if: The display does not match the documented no-tote pattern.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If a robot does not have a tote it will say layer_1, and the brown box is not shown.

steps.title: Record the observed tote state using documented meanings only
steps.purpose: Ensure the observation is documented without adding unsupported interpretation or actions.
steps.instruction: Record the observed tote state using only the documented meanings from the source: tote present, '?' indicating robot removed but tote not removed, or no tote indicated by 'layer_1' and no brown box.
steps.expected_result: The tote state is documented using only source-supported meanings.
steps.healthy_condition: The recorded state matches one of the documented patterns exactly.
steps.failure_condition: The observed state cannot be mapped to a documented pattern or requires undocumented corrective guidance.

actions.label: Document tote state
actions.exact_action: Record the observed tote state using only the documented meanings from the source.

stop_or_escalate_if: The tote display does not match any of the documented patterns.
A corrective action is needed that is not provided by this source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: If the robot has a tote it will show under the robot ID. If the tote has a '?' before it, the robot has been removed but the tote has not. If a robot does not have a tote it will say layer_1, and the brown box is not shown.

success_criteria: The selected robot's tote state is interpreted as one of the documented conditions.
A displayed tote ID is recognized as tote present.
A '?' prefix is recognized as meaning the robot was removed but the tote was not.
The combination of 'layer_1' and no brown box is recognized as no tote.
healthy_conditions: Robot details are visible after selecting a robot.
Tote text under the robot ID is readable.
The brown box visual and tote text align with one of the documented patterns.
failure_conditions: The robot detail panel cannot be viewed.
The tote display does not match any documented pattern.
The visual indicator and text indicator conflict.
A corrective action is needed but is not provided by this source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the tote display does not match any of the documented patterns.
Escalate if the robot detail panel cannot be accessed or interpreted.
Do not invent corrective actions beyond the documented meaning that a '?' indicates the robot was removed but the tote was not.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot selection detail panel, tote text under robot ID, '?' prefix, and brown box tote indicator.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: If the robot has a tote it will show under the robot ID. If the tote has a '?' before it, the robot has been removed but the tote has not. If a robot does not have a tote it will say layer_1, and the brown box is not shown.

missing_details: The source does not provide a corrective or recovery procedure for the '?' tote mismatch condition.
The source does not provide timing estimates.
The source does not define additional role boundaries beyond operator use of the display.
The source does not provide commands or system actions beyond visual interpretation.
